error.signature: Notion API returns 400 error or silently truncates content when block has too much text

verdict.summary: Notion API limits rich text per block to 2000 characters. A single paragraph block cannot exceed this. The API either returns 400 or silently truncates. Also, page content is limited to 100 blocks per API call (pagination required).

dead_ends.action: Send a large text block assuming it will be stored as-is
dead_ends.why_fails: Notion truncates at 2000 characters per rich text array element and 100 blocks per append call. Large documents are silently cut off.

dead_ends.action: Append all blocks in a single API call
dead_ends.why_fails: Maximum 100 blocks per append_block_children call. Documents with more blocks need paginated appends.

workarounds.action: Split text into 2000-character chunks across multiple paragraph blocks
workarounds.how: chunks = [text[i:i+2000] for i in range(0, len(text), 2000)]; blocks = [{'paragraph': {'rich_text': [{'text': {'content': c}}]}} for c in chunks]

workarounds.action: Batch block appends in groups of 100
workarounds.how: for i in range(0, len(blocks), 100): notion.blocks.children.append(page_id, children=blocks[i:i+100])

workarounds.action: Use Notion's toggle blocks for very long content to keep pages manageable
workarounds.how: Wrap long content sections in toggle blocks. Each toggle can contain sub-blocks with their own 2000-char limits.
